g.data-narration: Welcome to Input Devices - learn how humans communicate with computers
Input devices convert human-readable data into machine-readable form for the computer to process and understand
g.text: Hardware components that convert user data and commands into
machine-readable form for processing by the computer's processor.
Without input devices, computers cannot receive instructions or data.

g.data-narration: Input devices are essential because they bridge human intent with computer action, enabling all digital interactions
g.text: • Bridge human intent with computer action
• Enable all digital interactions and commands
• Evolved from mechanical switches to biometric scanners

g.data-narration: Keyboards are the most common text input devices with 104-108 keys, including mechanical, membrane, and virtual types
g.text: Most common text input device with 104-108 keys (QWERTY layout)

g.data-narration: Pointing devices control cursor position - mouse uses optical sensors, trackballs use rotating balls, touchpads use capacitive sensors
Scanning devices convert physical documents to digital format - flatbed, sheet-fed, OCR, and OMR each serve specific purposes
Barcode readers use laser/camera to read product codes. QR codes are 2D matrices holding more data than linear barcodes
g.text: Camera imaging • 2D matrix • More data storage

g.data-narration: Microphones convert sound waves to electrical signals for voice communication, recording, and speech recognition applications
Touch screens combine display and input - resistive needs pressure, capacitive needs conductive touch, infrared uses light beams, SAW uses ultrasonic waves
Biometric devices verify identity using unique physiological characteristics - fingerprints, iris patterns, and facial features provide secure authentication
g.text: Verify identity using unique physiological or behavioral characteristics

g.data-narration: Graphic tablets enable precise digital drawing with pressure sensitivity. Light pens detect screen light for direct drawing - now largely replaced by touch screens
g.text: Stylus with pressure/tilt • CAD, digital art • Natural drawing
Detects screen light • Early GUIs • Now replaced by touch

g.data-narration: Motion sensors detect 3D movement - accelerometers measure acceleration, gyroscopes detect rotation, depth cameras capture spatial information for gesture control
Different input devices select text differently - mouse uses click-drag, touchpad uses tap-hold-gesture, keyboard uses shift-arrows for selection
Retail barcode systems scan products, decode the unique code, and update inventory databases in real-time for efficient stock management
Biometric attendance uses fingerprint scanning, extracts ridge patterns into a template, and matches against stored templates for verification
Input devices convert human data to machine-readable form - keyboards for text, pointing devices for cursor control, scanners for digitization, biometrics for security
g.text: Touch screens combine display + input • Selection depends on application requirements
